briefing.headline: Rig up a Spinnerbait — finesse winter approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Spinnerbait is a strong choice for winter with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramount' in cold winter months.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Slow rolling a spinnerbait around steep rocky banks and laydowns in cold water. First Bassmaster win was in December at Lake Lanier doing exactly this. Use a plastic trailer — 'absolutely paramou
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a heavier 3/4-ounce spinnerbait in windy conditions — easier to cast and the positive blade resistance makes it easier to feel bites than finesse presentations
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Wheeler: The 7'1" MH casting rod is great for jigs, spinnerbaits, and single-hook baits around tight cover like docks, laydowns, and overhangs; the 7'4" MH moderate-fast casting rod is great for offshore cranking and big swimbaits with its parabol...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal winter is the toughest bite of the year. Fish retreat to the deepest structure available (20-35ft+) and barely move. Target steep structure where fish can access deep water without traveling — bluffs, vertical channel walls, bridge pilings.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
